description: Engine Foundation & Player - Vite + Three.js + TypeScript game engine with ECS architecture, player movement, isometric camera, character creation, WorkOS auth, and Neon DB setup

userStories.title: Project scaffolding with Vite + TypeScript + Three.js
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a properly configured Vite + TypeScript + Three.js project so that I have a working dev environment with linting, type checking, and hot reload.
acceptanceCriteria: Initialize project with pnpm and Vite TypeScript template
Install three and @types/three as dependencies
tsconfig.json configured with strict mode enabled
ESLint configured with TypeScript rules
Prettier configured for consistent formatting
pnpm dev starts dev server and renders a basic Three.js scene (colored box on a ground plane)
pnpm lint, pnpm typecheck, and pnpm build all pass
.env.example created with placeholder env vars: VITE_WORKOS_CLIENT_ID, VITE_WORKOS_REDIRECT_URI, VITE_NEON_DATA_API_URL
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Core engine types and centralized config
userStories.description: As a developer, I need shared type definitions, game event types, and a centralized config file so that all modules reference a single source of truth for game constants.
acceptanceCriteria: src/types.ts defines: GameAction enum (Jump, Interact, ToggleMap, ToggleDebug, Pause), CatType enum (Loaf, Zoomies, CuriosityCat, Pounce), TerrainType enum, GameEvent discriminated union type, Vector3-compatible types

src/config.ts contains all GDD constants: movement speeds (walk 4.5 u/s), jump impulse (3.5u), gravity (-12 u/s^2), coyote time (5 frames), jump buffer (5 frames), collision radius (0.4u), acceleration (0.3s), deceleration (0.2s), swimming speeds, oxygen rates, camera angles (45deg azimuth, 60deg elevation), auto-save interval (30s)
Config exported as frozen const with a runtime mutable copy for debug menu use
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: EventBus typed pub/sub system
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a typed event bus so that modules communicate without direct coupling.
acceptanceCriteria: src/engine/EventBus.ts implements: emit<T>(event), on<T>(type, handler): Unsubscribe, off(type, handler)
Events are type-safe — TypeScript enforces correct event shapes at call sites using the GameEvent union from types.ts
Supports multiple listeners per event type
Unsubscribe function returned by on() removes that specific listener
No memory leaks — destroyed modules can clean up subscriptions
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: SceneManager — Three.js isolation boundary
userStories.description: As a developer, I need Three.js isolated behind a single module so that no game logic ever imports from three directly.
acceptanceCriteria: src/engine/SceneManager.ts exposes: constructor(canvas), addMesh(config): SceneHandle, removeMesh(handle), updateTransform(handle, position, rotation, scale), render(), resize(width, height), screenToWorld(screenX, screenY): Vector3 | null
MeshConfig type supports geometry types: box, sphere, cylinder, plane; plus size, color (hex string), castShadow, receiveShadow
Internally creates THREE.Scene, THREE.WebGLRenderer, THREE.AmbientLight, THREE.DirectionalLight
Handles window resize events (updates renderer size and camera aspect)
Handles device pixel ratio for sharp rendering
SceneHandle is an opaque type — callers cannot access the underlying THREE.Object3D
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: ECS World, Entity, Component, and System base types
userStories.description: As a developer, I need an Entity-Component-System foundation so that all game objects are data-driven and systems operate on component queries.
acceptanceCriteria: src/ecs/Entity.ts — Entity is a numeric ID type (not a class with methods)
src/ecs/Component.ts — Base component type/interface with a static type tag for identification
src/ecs/System.ts — System interface with update(world, dt) method

src/ecs/World.ts implements: createEntity(): Entity, destroyEntity(entity), addComponent<T>(entity, component), removeComponent<T>(entity, componentType), getComponent<T>(entity, type): T | null, query(...componentTypes): Entity[]
Query results are cached — second call with same component types is O(1)
Cache invalidated when components are added/removed
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: ECS core components — Transform, Renderable, Velocity, Collider, PlayerControlled
userStories.description: As a developer, I need core ECS components so that entities can have position, visuals, physics, and player control.
acceptanceCriteria: src/ecs/components/Transform.ts — position (x,y,z), rotation (y-axis radians), scale (x,y,z)
src/ecs/components/Renderable.ts — meshConfig (geometry, size, color), sceneHandle (nullable, set by RenderSystem)
src/ecs/components/Velocity.ts — dx, dy, dz (units per second)
src/ecs/components/Collider.ts — shape (box/sphere/cylinder), size, isStatic, isTrigger, collisionLayer, collisionMask
src/ecs/components/PlayerControlled.ts — marker component with isGrounded, coyoteTimer, jumpBufferTimer fields
All components in src/ecs/components/ directory
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: RenderSystem — ECS to Three.js bridge
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a system that synchronizes ECS entity transforms to Three.js scene objects each frame.
acceptanceCriteria: src/systems/RenderSystem.ts implements System interface
Each frame: queries entities with Transform + Renderable components
For new entities (no sceneHandle yet): calls sceneManager.addMesh() using Renderable.meshConfig, stores handle on Renderable
For existing entities: calls sceneManager.updateTransform() with current Transform values
For destroyed entities (handle exists but entity removed): calls sceneManager.removeMesh() to clean up
No game logic in this system — pure data sync between ECS and Three.js
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: InputManager — keyboard and mouse intent abstraction
userStories.description: As a player, I want responsive keyboard and mouse input so that my character reacts immediately to WASD movement, jumping, and other actions.
acceptanceCriteria: src/engine/InputManager.ts exposes: getMovementIntent(): {x, z} (normalized), isActionPressed(action): boolean (frame-edge), isActionHeld(action): boolean, getMouseWorldPosition(): Vector3 | null, poll() (called once per frame)
WASD and Arrow Keys produce 8-directional movement intent
Diagonal movement normalized to unit length (no faster diagonal movement)
Jump (Spacebar) registers as single press (not continuous while held)
Interact (E), Map toggle (M), Debug toggle (Ctrl+D) register as single press
Mouse position raycasted to world-space ground plane via SceneManager.screenToWorld()
Browser default scroll on arrow keys prevented with e.preventDefault()
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: PhysicsEngine — gravity, ground detection, and collision bodies
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a lightweight physics engine for gravity, ground detection, and collision so that the player interacts physically with the world.
acceptanceCriteria: src/engine/PhysicsEngine.ts exposes: addBody(entity, config): BodyHandle, removeBody(handle), raycast(origin, direction, maxDist): RaycastHit | null, step(dt)
BodyConfig: shape (box|sphere|cylinder), size (Vector3), isStatic (bool), isTrigger (bool), collisionLayer (number), collisionMask (number)
Gravity applied at -12 u/s^2 to non-static non-grounded bodies
Ground detection via downward raycast with 0.05u snap tolerance
Circle collision (radius 0.4u) with 0.1u skin width for wall sliding
Trigger volumes (isTrigger=true) detect overlap but don't block movement — emit events via EventBus
Static bodies (terrain, walls) never move
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: CollisionSystem — per-frame broad and narrow phase
userStories.description: As a developer, I need per-frame collision detection so that entities interact physically with terrain and each other.
acceptanceCriteria: src/systems/CollisionSystem.ts implements System interface
Queries entities with Transform + Collider each frame
Broad phase: AABB overlap detection to find candidate pairs
Narrow phase: circle-circle or circle-box resolution
Collision response: pushes non-static bodies apart (wall sliding with skin width)
Trigger overlaps detected and emitted via EventBus (no physics response)
Collision layers/masks respected (e.g., player collides with terrain but not with UI triggers)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: MovementSystem — walk, jump, coyote time, jump buffer
userStories.description: As a player, I want smooth walking and responsive jumping so that controlling my character feels satisfying.
acceptanceCriteria: src/systems/MovementSystem.ts implements System interface
Walk speed: 4.5 u/s from config.ts
Acceleration: 0.3s ramp-up from 0 to walk speed; Deceleration: 0.2s ramp-down
Jump: 3.5u upward impulse on Spacebar press, 1.2u apex height
Air control: 70% horizontal movement retention while airborne
Coyote time: player can still jump for 5 frames (~83ms) after walking off an edge
Jump buffer: if Spacebar pressed within 5 frames before landing, jump executes on land
Character rotation: mesh faces movement direction (8-directional)
Movement reads from InputManager.getMovementIntent() — no direct keyboard access
All constants sourced from config.ts runtime copy (debug-tunable)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: CameraController — isometric follow with dynamic offset
userStories.description: As a player, I want an isometric camera that smoothly follows my character with a slight lead in my movement direction.
acceptanceCriteria: src/engine/CameraController.ts exposes: follow(target: Entity), setMode(mode: 'follow' | 'free'), update(dt)
Uses Three.js OrthographicCamera positioned at 45deg azimuth, 60deg elevation per GDD spec
Dynamic offset: camera leads 2-3 units in the player's facing direction (lerped smoothly)
Smooth follow with damping (no jitter, no snapping)
Scroll wheel adjusts zoom level
Camera clamps to map boundaries (doesn't show void beyond map edges)
Free mode: WASD moves camera directly (for map editor use later)
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: WorkOS AuthKit authentication integration
userStories.description: As a user, I want to sign in with my account so that my character and progress are saved.
acceptanceCriteria: @workos-inc/authkit-react installed and configured
src/main.ts wraps app in AuthKitProvider with clientId and redirectUri from env vars
src/App.ts acts as auth gate: unauthenticated shows title screen with Sign In button; authenticated shows game
src/modules/auth/AuthModule.ts wraps WorkOS hook: signIn(), signOut(), getUser(): AuthUser | null, getToken(): Promise<string>
Sign in redirects to WorkOS Hosted UI and returns with valid JWT
Session persists across page refresh (HttpOnly cookie refresh token)
Sign out clears session and returns to title screen
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Drizzle schema and Neon DB configuration
userStories.description: As a developer, I need the database schema defined and deployed so that user data can be persisted.
acceptanceCriteria: drizzle/schema.ts defines tables: characters (user_id TEXT PK, shape TEXT, color_hex TEXT, size_scale NUMERIC, timestamps), game_saves (user_id TEXT PK, version TEXT, save_data JSONB, timestamps), debug_overrides (user_id TEXT PK, overrides JSONB)
drizzle.config.ts configured for Neon PostgreSQL connection using DATABASE_URL env var
drizzle/rls-policies.sql contains RLS setup: enable pg_session_jwt extension, enable RLS on all tables, create policies with user_id = auth.user_id()
drizzle-orm and drizzle-kit installed as devDependencies (not bundled in browser)
@neondatabase/serverless installed as devDependency for migration tooling
Schema deployable via pnpm drizzle-kit push
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: NeonClient — fetch-based Data API access
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a client module to read and write data via Neon Data API so that the browser communicates with the database directly.
acceptanceCriteria: src/modules/persistence/NeonClient.ts implements fetch()-based DB access using Neon Data API PostgREST endpoint
Constructor takes apiUrl (from VITE_NEON_DATA_API_URL) and getToken function (from AuthModule)
upsert(table, data) method: PUT with Prefer: resolution=merge-duplicates header and Bearer token
select<T>(table, query?) method: GET with optional PostgREST query params and Bearer token
delete(table, query?) method: DELETE with Bearer token
All requests include Authorization: Bearer <jwt> header
Error handling: throws on non-2xx responses with descriptive error message
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: MapData types and MapManager module
userStories.description: As a developer, I need map data types and a manager to load maps from data definitions so that maps are data-driven.
acceptanceCriteria: src/maps/MapData.ts defines MapData interface: name, size {width, depth}, terrain (2D array of TerrainCell), waterZones array, spawnPoints array, hiddenTerrain array
TerrainCell type: type (grass/dirt/stone), height (float), navigable (bool)
SpawnPoint type: id, x, z, type ('player' | 'cat' | 'resource' | 'yarn')
src/game/MapManager.ts exposes: loadMap(data: MapData), getTerrainAt(x, z): TerrainType, getHeightAt(x, z): number, getSpawnPoints(): SpawnPoint[]
loadMap() creates terrain entities with Transform, Renderable, Collider — rendered as colored planes/boxes per terrain type
Map boundary walls created as static colliders at map edges
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Test map definition
userStories.description: As a player, I want to spawn on a test map with varied terrain so that I have a world to explore.
acceptanceCriteria: src/maps/TestMap.ts exports a MapData definition for a 60x60 unit map
Map contains: grass zones (green), dirt zone (brown), stone area (gray)
Water zone defined: ~15x15u in south-east, 12u deep
Elevated platforms: 2-3 blocks at 2-3u height (for testing jump and future Pounce)
Player spawn point at map center
2 cat spawn points in north and south areas
Hidden terrain area in east (invisible, connected via bridge/platform shape)
8-12 grass resource spawn points scattered across grass zones
3-5 sticks resource spawn points in forest/north zone
1-2 water resource spawn points near water zone
2-3 yarn pickup spawn points scattered around map
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Game orchestrator — bootstrap and game loop
userStories.description: As a developer, I need a top-level Game class that bootstraps all modules and runs the game loop.
acceptanceCriteria: src/engine/Game.ts exposes: constructor(canvas: HTMLCanvasElement), start(): Promise<void>, pause(), resume()
start() initializes all modules in correct dependency order: EventBus, SceneManager, InputManager, PhysicsEngine, World, all Systems, CameraController, MapManager, UIManager
Game loop uses fixed timestep (1/60s physics step) with accumulator pattern and interpolation for rendering
Frame update order: InputManager.poll() -> MovementSystem -> PhysicsEngine.step() -> CollisionSystem -> CameraSystem -> RenderSystem -> UIManager -> SceneManager.render()
pause() stops the loop; resume() restarts it
App.ts integrates Game: after auth, creates Game with canvas element, calls start()
Typecheck passes

userStories.title: Character creator UI and entity spawning
userStories.description: As a new player, I want to customize my character's shape, color, and size so that my avatar feels personal.
acceptanceCriteria: src/ui/CharacterCreatorUI.ts renders an HTML overlay with: shape picker (Block, Sphere, Cylinder with visual labels), color picker (hex input or palette of 8+ preset colors), size selector (Small 0.8, Medium 1.0, Large 1.2), Start Game button
src/game/CharacterCreator.ts takes chosen appearance and creates player entity with matching Renderable (shape/color/size) and Transform at map spawn point
Character appearance saved to Neon DB characters table on creation via NeonClient
On subsequent logins, character loaded from DB — skip creator and spawn directly
Character renders in scene as colored Three.js primitive matching chosen options
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill

userStories.title: Basic HUD shell — health and FPS
userStories.description: As a player, I need a minimal HUD showing my basic status so I have feedback during gameplay.
acceptanceCriteria: src/ui/UIManager.ts manages mounting/unmounting UI panels as DOM elements over the Three.js canvas
src/ui/HUD.ts renders: health display (5 placeholder hearts), FPS counter (top-right, only in dev builds via import.meta.env.DEV)
HUD is plain DOM manipulation (no React) — updated via UIManager.update(dt) each frame
HUD elements positioned at screen edges to not obstruct center gameplay
Typecheck passes
Verify in browser using dev-browser skill
